goal: Download and crop February 4, 2024 Sentinel TCI route-window strips after the SCL probe looked marginally competitive with March 20.

method.coordinate_transform: Reuse the same draft-bank native-CRS window math validated for the March 20 review.
method.pixel_model: Sentinel-2 TCI uses 10 m pixels at 10980x10980 on the same tile origins as the companion SCL QA.
method.clip_algorithm: Crop the already computed draft-bank pixel windows from full TCI COGs and downsample each strip to 1024 px wide review PNGs.
limitations: The source bank geometry is generated from the Unreal preview scaffold and is not official Costa Rica hydrography, surveyed bank geometry, wetted width, or protected-area-safe publication geometry.
The TCI strips are not masked by SCL; the companion SCL strips remain QA references only.
The clips are narrow bbox strips around draft banks, not a polygon route clip, stitched mosaic, source drape, material input, or mask source.

outputs.visual_review: The route-context strip has visible cloud and shadow clusters across the left, middle, and right portions of the draft-bank window, so it cannot replace the Pacuare source drape.
The narrower 16PHR strip still contains visible cloud and shadow over the draft corridor and remains review-only.

windowed_read_decision.decision: February 4 TCI strips confirm the marginal SCL candidate is still visibly cloud/shadow contaminated in the draft-bank route context. Do not promote this scene to Pacuare source drape, masks, materials, or lifelike screenshot evidence.

forbidden_use: production water or vegetation mask generation
promotion_blockers: Find cleaner same-date coverage or prove exact route-window SCL masking leaves enough usable corridor texture.
Replace preview-scaffold bank geometry with official or guide-approved route/bank geometry.
Stitch only after exact clipping, SCL/cloud/shadow QA, seam review, scale review, and terms review pass.
Record guide/geospatial approval before any imagery-derived Pacuare material or lifelike capture claim.
